advantages: Provably optimal whenever the problem has optimal substructure (unlike greedy heuristics).
Collapses exponential recursion to polynomial via memoization or tabulation.
Two implementation styles — pick whichever reads better.
Tables often compress (rolling rows, state pruning) to reduce memory further.
disadvantages: Polynomial doesn't mean fast — a O(n²) or O(n·W) DP can still be too slow at production scale.
Memory cost of the table can dominate; must consider compression.
Designing the state and recurrence is the hard part — much harder than greedy.
Doesn't apply when sub-problems don't overlap (use divide-and-conquer) or when constraints are non-local (use search).
